title: ceanothus americanus var. intermedius
intro: Ceanothus americanus var. intermedius, also known as New Jersey tea, is a shrub native to North America, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a substitute for black tea during the American Revolution due to its caffeine-free and astringent properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Ceanothus americanus var. intermedius can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, tinctures, and teas, as well as dried leaves, which can be used in capsules or as a topical application.
